tasks.task_name: Find warm backpacking destination (1-2 night, mid-March)
tasks.first_domino: Google 'warm backpacking destinations mid-March weekend trips'
tasks.hook_line: Where can you hike in a t-shirt in mid-March while everyone else is still freezing?
tasks.reward: Locks in the destination so you can book flights and tell Mariano the plan
nuggets: Big Bend TX hits 75°F mid-March
Red Rock Canyon NV camping open year-round
Joshua Tree permits available online
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find a warm backpacking destination for a 1-2 night trip the weekend of March 14-15, 2026. I'm celebrating my friend Mariano's 30th birthday with a backpacking trip before we head to Chicago. We need somewhere warm (ideally 60-75°F during the day), beginner-to-intermediate trails, and accessible from major airports since we're flying in. Give me 3-5 options with trail recommendations, expected weather, permit requirements, and nearest airports. Include a mix of national parks and lesser-known spots.

tasks.task_name: Figure out REI gear rentals (backpack, tent, sleeping bag)
tasks.first_domino: Go to rei.com and click 'Rentals' in top nav
tasks.hook_line: REI rents full backpacking kits — tent, pack, sleeping bag, the works.
tasks.reward: Answers the gear question without dropping $800 on equipment you'll use once
nuggets: REI Adventure Kit rents for ~$50/weekend
Includes backpack, tent, sleeping bag, pad
Pick up locally or ship 5 days before
tasks.system_prompt: I'm planning a 1-2 night backpacking trip in mid-March and need to rent gear from REI. I need a backpack, tent (2-person), sleeping bag, and sleeping pad. Research REI's rental options: what packages they offer, pricing, pickup/return process, availability for March 14-15, and whether I should reserve now or closer to the trip. Also check if there are better alternatives to REI for first-time backpacking gear rentals.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights and search nearest airport to destination
tasks.hook_line: 7 days out and no flights booked — prices are climbing fast.
tasks.reward: Gets you and Mariano to the trailhead on time
nuggets: Book Fri Mar 13 morning, return Sun Mar 15 evening
Compare Phoenix, Las Vegas, El Paso airports
Southwest often cheapest for last-minute trips
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book flights for a backpacking trip March 14-15, 2026. Flying out Friday March 13 (morning preferred) and returning Sunday March 15 (evening preferred). I haven't finalized the destination yet, but likely options include Big Bend TX (El Paso airport), Joshua Tree CA (Palm Springs or Las Vegas), or Red Rock Canyon NV (Las Vegas). Show me flight options from San Francisco to each of these airports, compare prices, and recommend the best timing. I'll be traveling with one friend.

tasks.task_name: Book flight from backpacking to Chicago
tasks.first_domino: Search flights backpacking-airport to Chicago on March 15
tasks.hook_line: Need to land in Chicago Sunday night to make Monday work — no flexibility.
tasks.reward: Completes the full birthday weekend logistics chain
nuggets: ORD and MDW both options for Chicago
Red-eye arrival Monday AM also workable
United often has best connections from Southwest hubs
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book a flight from my backpacking destination to Chicago for Mariano's 30th birthday. Departing Sunday March 15 evening (after returning from the backpacking trip) and arriving in Chicago that night or early Monday March 16. The backpacking location isn't finalized yet but will likely be El Paso TX, Las Vegas NV, or Palm Springs CA. Show me flight options from each to Chicago (ORD or MDW), compare prices and timing, and recommend the best connection. I'll be traveling solo on this leg.

tasks.first_domino: Text Mariano and ask if he wants gifts or experiences
tasks.hook_line: What do you get someone who just organized a backpacking trip for their own birthday?
tasks.reward: Shows up with something thoughtful instead of empty-handed
nuggets: Chicago has great vintage shops for unique finds
Backpacking gear could double as practical + symbolic
Nice whiskey bottle for Chicago celebration
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out a 30th birthday gift for my friend Mariano. We're doing a backpacking trip together March 14-15 before his actual birthday on March 19 in Chicago. He's outdoorsy, enjoys good food and drinks, and this is a milestone birthday. Budget around $50-100. Give me 5-7 ideas that range from practical (gear he might actually use) to memorable (something specific to the trip or Chicago). I want to avoid generic gifts.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Call insurance company and ask 'foreign prescription reimbursement policy'
tasks.hook_line: Can you hack vision insurance to save $400+ on Japanese eyeglasses?
tasks.reward: Know exactly what documentation you need before walking into a Kyoto shop
nuggets: US prescription valid in Japan for 90 days
Most US insurance reimburses foreign eyewear with receipt
Bring paper prescription copy + PD measurement
tasks.system_prompt: I'm buying 4 pairs of prescription eyeglasses in Japan (March 23 - April 7, 2026) to save money compared to US prices. I need to know: (1) Can I use my US prescription in Japan or do I need a new eye exam there? (2) Will my US vision insurance reimburse me for glasses purchased abroad, and if so, what documentation do I need to collect? (3) What's the typical process for buying prescription glasses in Japan as a tourist? Research these questions and give me a checklist of what to bring and what to ask my insurance company.

tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Google 'best eyeglasses shops Kyoto' and open Maps
tasks.hook_line: Where do Kyoto locals go for affordable, stylish glasses?
tasks.reward: Knocks out eyeglasses shopping early in the trip so you're not hauling them everywhere
nuggets: Jins and Zoff popular chains, ~¥5000/pair
Nishiki Market area has boutique optical shops
OWNDAYS offers 30-minute turnaround on prescriptions
tasks.system_prompt: I'm visiting Kyoto March 25-27, 2026 and want to buy 4 pairs of prescription eyeglasses early in the trip. Research the best eyeglasses stores in Kyoto: (1) Chain stores like Jins, Zoff, OWNDAYS with pricing and prescription turnaround times, (2) Boutique shops for unique frames, (3) Locations near central Kyoto hotels. I want a mix of affordable everyday pairs and one or two stylish statement pieces. Give me a list of 5-7 shops with Google Maps links, price ranges, and what makes each worth visiting.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples walking route'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free entry, open 24hrs
Arashiyama bamboo grove best before 9am
Kinkaku-ji ¥500 entry, opens 9am
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto (March 25-27, 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, and good food. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with opening hours, Google Maps links, and a suggested walking route. Include a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) and less touristy spots. Also suggest 3-4 restaurants for dinner. Keep it realistic — we're not trying to see everything.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'things to do Takayama' and open Google Maps
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the Japan Alps base camp — how much can you explore in 1.5 days?
tasks.reward: Gives you a mountain-town break between temple cities
nuggets: Morning markets open 7am-noon daily
Shirakawa-go village 50min bus, UNESCO site
Old town Sanmachi district free to walk
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 1.5 days in Takayama, Japan (late March 2026). Traveling with Dana. We're staying overnight and want a mix of outdoor activities (hiking, nature) and cultural experiences (old town, markets). Give me a day-by-day itinerary including: (1) Morning markets and old town walking, (2) Day trip options like Shirakawa-go or nearby hikes, (3) Dinner restaurant recommendations. Include transportation details, opening hours, and Google Maps links. We're coming from Kyoto and heading to Tokyo after.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Tokyo neighborhoods guide' and open Maps
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive — how do you explore without drowning in options?
tasks.reward: Closes out all activity planning for the entire 2-week trip
nuggets: Shibuya crossing best seen from Starbucks 2nd floor
Tsukiji Outer Market breakfast 5am-1pm
TeamLab Borderless tickets sell out weeks ahead
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 4-5 days in Tokyo (early April 2026). Traveling with Dana. We like food, walking, quirky neighborhoods, and some modern/tech experiences. Already have hotels booked. Give me a day-by-day itinerary covering: (1) Major neighborhoods to explore (Shibuya, Harajuku, Asakusa, etc), (2) Food experiences (Tsukiji market, ramen spots, izakayas), (3) 2-3 'Tokyo-specific' experiences (TeamLab, arcade, observation deck), (4) Stationery/fountain pen shops for shopping. Include opening hours, Google Maps links, and realistic walking routes. Don't overschedule — we want time to wander.

tasks.first_domino: Open VS Code project folder and run 'npm run dev'
tasks.hook_line: You've got the foundation — now build the thing that makes Cursor actually useful.
tasks.reward: Gets you closer to replacing the clunky Craft agent workflow
nuggets: Extension API documentation at code.visualstudio.com/api
Cursor uses LSP for context — hook into same protocol
Webview panels for task visualization UI
tasks.system_prompt: I'm building a VS Code extension called AI Task Pilot to help with AI-assisted development while maintaining task focus. The goal is to visualize work in progress and provide AI with context of ongoing work while editing a shared document locally. It's an alternative to my Craft agent system which doesn't integrate well with Cursor projects. Help me continue development on the core features: (1) Task state management and persistence, (2) Document context tracking, (3) AI context injection. I'm using TypeScript and the VS Code Extension API. Show me code examples and architectural patterns for these features.

tasks.first_domino: Sketch 3 different task panel layouts on paper
tasks.hook_line: How do you show task context without cluttering the editor?
tasks.reward: Creates the UI that makes the extension actually usable
nuggets: VS Code webview panels support React
Sidebar placement keeps tasks visible but non-intrusive
Tailwind CSS works in webview context
tasks.system_prompt: I'm designing the UI for a VS Code extension that visualizes work-in-progress tasks and AI context. The extension needs to show: (1) Current task focus, (2) Related files/context, (3) AI conversation history. It should be minimally intrusive — visible but not distracting. Help me design the interface: should it be a sidebar panel, a floating widget, or something else? Show me examples of well-designed VS Code extension UIs and suggest specific UI patterns for displaying tasks, context, and AI interactions. Consider ADHD-friendly design principles.

tasks.first_domino: Research VS Code Language Server Protocol documentation
tasks.hook_line: Can you give AI the right context without manually copying files every time?
tasks.reward: Makes Cursor conversations actually useful by auto-loading relevant context
nuggets: LSP provides file dependency graphs automatically
Cursor API can accept context via clipboard or files
VS Code workspace API tracks open editors and changes
tasks.system_prompt: I'm building an AI context management system for a VS Code extension. The goal is to automatically provide AI assistants (like Cursor) with relevant context about the current task without manual file copying. The system needs to: (1) Track which files are relevant to the current task, (2) Monitor changes and update context dynamically, (3) Format context in a way AI can easily consume. Help me architect this system using VS Code Extension APIs. Show me how to track file dependencies, watch for changes, and integrate with Cursor or other AI coding tools.

recommended_slots.reason: 7 days to trip, early morning, perfect for low-pressure research before the day starts
Quick coordination task that unblocks eyeglasses shopping — fits ramp-up energy
15-minute palette cleanser, novelty factor, gets gear question answered
